questions.question: The state of matter which has a definite volume but no definite shape.
questions.question_html: The state of matter which has a definite volume but no definite shape.
questions.explanation: The state of matter which has a definite volume but no definite shape is called as liquid. It takes the shape of the container which it occupies. Liquids are not easily compressible.

questions.question_html: As you cannot see through objects made from material, such materials are called

questions.question: If a substance can be beaten into thin sheet, it is called
questions.question_html: If a substance can be beaten into thin sheet, it is called
questions.explanation: If a substance can be beaten into thin sheet by hammering, the substance is called malleable. Malleability is a property of metals to deform under compression. Some of the examples are gold, silver, tin etc

Help Boojho in finding out the material(s) which is/are opaque.

questions.question: While doing an activity in class, the teacher asked paheli to handover a transluscent material. Which among the following material will paheli pick and give her teacher?
questions.question_html: While doing an activity in class, the teacher asked paheli to handover a transluscent material. Which among the following material will paheli pick and give her teacher?

questions.question: Which of the following materials is not lustrous?
questions.question_html: Which of the following materials is not lustrous?
questions.explanation: Those substances that have shining surface are called as lustrous substance. Diamond, gold, and silver are lustrous while wood is not lustrous. Metals are lustrous as they have a smooth surface and reflect light while non-metal like wood have a rough surface and cannot reflect light. Hence, they are not lustrous.

questions.question: Which pair of substances among the following would float in a tumbler half filled with water?
questions.question_html: Which pair of substances among the following would float in a tumbler half filled with water?

questions.question: Choose the opaque materials from the following
questions.question_html: Choose the opaque materials from the following
questions.explanation: Those materials through which light cannot pass is called as opaque materials. Wooden door is a opaque material. Opque materials casts shadow as it blocks the light completely. Some other examples are card board box, book etc.

questions.question: Select the one used as a basis for grouping Objects
questions.question_html: Select the one used as a basis for grouping Objects

options.text: shape of the objects and materials of which they are made
options.html: shape of the objects and materials of which they are made

options.text: materials of which they are made

questions.explanation: Objects can be grouped on the basis of their shape and also on the basis of the material of which they are made.
